samples.xml_description: Implement an unsigned 16-bit multiplier using shift and accumulate operations. Clock signal. Active-low reset signal. Chip enable signal to initiate multiplication. Multiplicand input. Multiplier input. Product output. Completion flag indicating the end of multiplication. Shift count register, 0 to 16. Update shift count register. if (!rst_n) i <= 0; else if (start && i < 17) i <= i + 1; else if (!start) i <= 0; Update completion flag. if (!rst_n) done_r <= 0; else if (i == 16) done_r <= 1; else if (i == 17) done_r <= 0; Update product register. if (!rst_n) yout_r <= 0; else if (start && i == 0) // Load operands begin areg <= ain; breg <= bin; yout_r <= 0; end else if (start && i > 0 && i < 17 && areg[i - 1]) // Shift and add yout_r <= (breg << (i - 1)) | (yout_r & ~((32 << (i - 1)) - 1)); The module performs unsigned 16-bit multiplication using shift and accumulate operations. It maintains a shift count (i) to control the multiplication process and a completion flag (done_r) to indicate when the multiplication is done. On each clock cycle, if the reset signal is low, it resets the shift count and the completion flag. If the shift count is less than 17 and the start signal is high, it increments the shift count. If the shift count is 0, it loads the multiplicand and multiplier into the registers and clears the product. For shift counts greater than 0, it accumulates the shifted multiplier value to the product if the corresponding bit in the multiplicand is set.
